records.ety: [OE. apparel, apareil, OF. apareil, appareil, preparation, provision, furniture, OF. apareiller to match, prepare, F. appareiller; OF. a (L. ad) + pareil like, similar, fr. LL. pariculus, dim. of L. par equal. See Pair.]

defs: External clothing; vesture; garments; dress; garb; external habiliments or array.
quotes: Fresh in his new apparel, proud and young.
At public devotion his resigned carriage made religion appear in the natural apparel of simplicity.

defs: A small ornamental piece of embroidery worn on albs and some other ecclesiastical vestments.
The furniture of a ship, as masts, sails, rigging, anchors, guns, etc.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Appareled, or Apparelled (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Appareling, or Apparelling.]

defs: To make or get (something) ready; to prepare.
To furnish with apparatus; to equip; to fit out.
To dress or clothe; to attire.
quotes: They which are gorgeously appareled, and live delicately, are in kings' courts.

defs: To dress with external ornaments; to cover with something ornamental; to deck; to embellish; as, trees appareled with flowers, or a garden with verdure.
